growthexperiments-desc: This extension contains experiments done by the Growth team.
growthexperiments-config-validator-datat: $1 is expected to be $2, but is $3
growthexperiments-config-validator-conte: Content-model mismatch: GrowthExperiments config file must have JSON content model, $1 detected
growthexperiments-config-validator-newco: Invalid task type handler ID: $1 is not a valid value
communityconfiguration-communityupdates-: Share important community news, campaigns, events, or WikiProjects on the newcomer homepage.

This [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Growth/Community_Updates|Community Updates]] module will display on the homepage and should be used to share important community news, campaigns, events, or WikiProjects. Priority should be given to updates that support the [https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Special:MyLanguage/Movement_Strategy/Recommendations/Identify_Topics_for_Impact#Changes_and_Actions Movement strategy’s “Topics for Impact”] recommendation.
A short title that best represents the campaign, project, event, or community initiative that is being highlighted.
A short and friendly informational message to spark interest.
Minimum number of edits a user must have to be shown this banner
Search for an image on Wikimedia Commons by typing its name and selecting it from the list. For the best display, choose an image with a square aspect ratio.
This can be a page on the local wiki, or an interwiki page like "meta:Event:Wikipedians_unite!"
Add a white background to the image in dark mode
If your image has a transparent background, it may be difficult to see in dark mode. If the text or icon is dark, adding a white background can ensure it remains visible in dark mode.
communityconfiguration-helppanel-descrip: Customize Help panel settings to align with your community's requirements and define resources within it to assist and guide new editors.
communityconfiguration-helppanel-info-te: The help panel can be available in any namespace, in reading or edit mode, as configured below. It includes the ability to search for help, click help links, and ask questions to either mentors or to the help desk. Links can be configured to be any links in the wiki, but recommendations are included below.
communityconfiguration-helppanel-gehelpp: Where should questions from the help panel be directed?
Where on the help desk page should new questions be posted?
Namespaces with the help panel disabled in both reading and editing mode
Namespaces where the help panel is available in reading mode
Namespaces to include in the help panel's free text search for help
Help desk page where questions should be posted from the help panel.
How to write a good article
Destination page for "View more help articles"
communityconfiguration-mentorship-descri: Customize mentorship settings and eligibility, while adjusting edit minimums and timeframes for mentors and praise-worthy mentees.
communityconfiguration-mentorship-info-t:
Newcomers are automatically assigned to mentors who are categorized as "Automatically assigned mentors" in the [[Special:ManageMentors|list of mentors]]. A mentor can also update their settings in the Mentor dashboard to only claim mentees manually, and then they are categorized there as "Manually assigned mentors". The ideal number of mentors is: one mentor for each group of 500 new accounts, with a minimum of 3 mentors. Mentees can also receive encouraging messages from established editors, which can improve new editor retention.

The Personalized praise module surfaces praiseworthy new editors to their mentor, to help productive new editors feel more engaged and involved in the community.
communityconfiguration-mentorship-gement: Editors who meet all eligibility criteria are automatically eligible to enroll as mentors

If this setting remains unchecked while Mentorship features are turned on, then potential mentors will be unable to enroll in Mentorship, unless they have the "enrollasmentor" right or are added by Admins as new Mentors via [[Special:ManageMentors]].
Minimum number of days a user must be registered to sign up as a mentor
Minimum number of edits a user must have made (on any namespace) to sign up as a mentor
communityconfiguration-mentorship-gepers: Number of days that are considered when evaluating mentee's praiseworthiness
Mentors can change this option for their own user in the Mentor dashboard.
By default, how often should mentors receive notifications to remind them to send encouragement to mentees who are editing successfully (in hours)?
Mentors can change this option for their own user in the Mentor dashboard.
Maximum number of edits an user can have to be considered praiseworthy
Default minimum number of edits a mentee must have to be praiseworthy
Mentors can change this option for their own user in the Mentor dashboard.
communityconfiguration-mentorship-gement: Opt out experienced users from mentorship
When enabled, mentees are automatically removed from mentorship after reaching both thresholds. They can still get a mentor from their Homepage.
Mentors will be marked as Away for up to 1 month, or until they reach the removal threshold.
Days of inactivity before marking as Away
Their mentees will be reassigned to other mentors.
communityconfiguration-growthhomepage-de: Personalize onboarding for new account holders via post-registration help and Leveling up notifications.
communityconfiguration-growthhomepage-in: The Newcomer homepage ([[Special:Homepage]]) provides guidance for new account holders. Upon registration, if a new account holder indicates they want to create an article or add a new image to Wikipedia, we recommend making a smaller edit first to get started and provide a link for further guidance.
communityconfiguration-growthhomepage-ge: Destination help for a link about creating a new article
Destination page for a link about how to add images in the visual editor

Maximum number of edits a newcomer can have to qualify for receiving this notification. If set to 0, no newcomers will receive this notification. The higher the number, the more newcomers will receive this notification. This notification is only sent to newcomers who have not completed Suggested edits.

Maximum number of suggested edits a newcomer can complete to qualify for receiving this notification. If set to 0, no newcomers will receive this notification. The higher the number, the more newcomers will receive this notification. This notification is only sent to newcomers who have already completed at least one Suggested edit.
communityconfiguration-growthsuggesteded: Suggested edits
Customize the newcomer homepage's Suggested edits feature.

Suggested Edits help newcomers get started editing. Newcomers on the web and mobile web have access to Suggested Edits via their Newcomer Homepage. Suggested Edits receive the “Newcomer task” tag.
Please note that updates to this form will not impact Suggested Edits within the Wikipedia iOS and Android Apps. Please reach out to the Apps team via [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Talk:Wikimedia_Apps Talk:Wikimedia Apps] to discuss mobile app features. Some types of Newcomer Tasks are surfaced through maintenance templates. Articles containing those templates are added to the Homepage's Suggested Edit feed. To see how many articles are available for each type of task, see [[Special:NewcomerTasksInfo]].
Infobox templates

These templates are needed for the [[mw:Special:MyLanguage/Growth/Personalized first day/Structured tasks/Add an image|image suggestions]] newcomer task feature. Articles containing these templates will not be listed as image suggestion tasks.
Copyedit
The task is disabled.
Copy editing: templates to find articles that need copy editing, such as spelling, grammar, and punctuation improvements.
Articles containing templates defined here will not be shown to users as tasks for this task type.
Articles containing categories defined here will not be shown to users as tasks for this task type.

Weight of underlinked articles

Affects how underlinked articles are prioritized. This field requires a percentage in its decimal form, so the number should be between 0 and 1. The more {{GENDER:|you}} increase this number, the more likely newcomers will receive underlinked article suggestions.
Minimum required link score

Minimum confidence threshold for link suggestions. This field requires a percentage in its decimal form, so the number should be between 0 and 1. If {{GENDER:|you}} increase the number, the suggestions presented to the end user will have a higher likelihood of being good suggestions, however fewer suggestions will be available. If you decrease the number, there will be more suggestions available but some will have a lower likelihood of being good quality suggestions.
Stop suggesting "Add a link" tasks based on newcomers' total edits
